message:
chore(compat): remove quic-compat and http3-compat feature flags from core

The legacy Tokio-backed compatibility adapters for QUIC and HTTP/3 now
live in the separate asupersync-tokio-compat crate. This removes:
- quic-compat and http3-compat feature declarations from Cargo.toml
- All cfg(feature) conditional module/re-export blocks in lib.rs,
  http/mod.rs, and net/mod.rs
- The compile_error! guards that blocked accidental enablement
- QuicIncoming from the native quic re-export (moved to compat)

No runtime behavior change — these code paths were already unreachable
since the features were never enabled in the workspace.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -93,10 +93,8 @@ mysql = []
 # These expose Tokio-free native paths for inventory/build/test contracts.
 quic = []
 http3 = ["quic"]
-# Legacy compatibility wrappers over upstream quinn/h3 stacks.
-# Kept explicitly separate from native rollout; disabled by default.
-quic-compat = []
-http3-compat = ["quic-compat"]
+# Legacy Tokio-backed compatibility wrappers live in the separate
+# `asupersync-tokio-compat` crate, not in the core feature graph.
 # Enable Kafka client integration via rdkafka.
 kafka = ["dep:rdkafka"]
 # Enable HTTP response compression (gzip, deflate) via flate2.

@@ -68,18 +68,6 @@
 #[cfg(test)]
 extern crate self as asupersync;
 
-#[cfg(feature = "quic-compat")]
-compile_error!(
-    "feature `quic-compat` is reserved for legacy quinn-backed adapters and is disabled \
-     in this Tokio-free core build."
-);
-
-#[cfg(feature = "http3-compat")]
-compile_error!(
-    "feature `http3-compat` is reserved for legacy h3/h3-quinn adapters and is disabled \
-     in this Tokio-free core build."
-);
-
 #[cfg(all(
     target_arch = "wasm32",
     not(any(
